source_scope: The 19 papers cited by the public ChatGPT ScopeDiff discussion; metadata verified against canonical arXiv records.

sources.title: Insertion Transformer: Flexible Sequence Generation via Insertion Operations
sources.summary: Introduces insertion-based generation with arbitrary orderings and parallel insertion at several positions.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Treat expansion as a first-class action instead of reserving a fixed token canvas.
metadata.repo_relevance: TopologyAction expansion and concurrent active scopes
metadata.limitations: The paper studies sequence translation rather than typed OpenUI trees.

sources.title: Levenshtein Transformer
sources.summary: Uses learned insertion and deletion operations for variable-length generation and iterative refinement.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Supervise expansion, deletion, and contraction as complementary edits.
metadata.limitations: Its sequence edit policy is not a typed-hole or grammar-interface model.

sources.title: MaskGIT: Masked Generative Image Transformer
sources.summary: Iteratively refines many masked positions in parallel with confidence-based scheduling.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Use parallel refinement and confidence scheduling, but replace image positions with typed scopes.
metadata.limitations: Image-token masking assumes a fixed canvas and carries no grammar interface.

sources.title: Simple and Effective Masked Diffusion Language Models
sources.summary: Shows that a simplified masked discrete-diffusion objective and training recipe can be competitive for language modeling.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Keep the base denoising objective simple while adding typed contract supervision separately.
metadata.limitations: The paper does not model changing AST topology or local semantic contracts.

sources.title: Diffusion Forcing: Next-token Prediction Meets Full-Sequence Diffusion
sources.summary: Trains sequence positions with independent noise levels to bridge causal prediction and full-sequence diffusion.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Sample independent noise for concurrently active scopes.
metadata.limitations: The reported domains and likelihood argument do not validate OpenUI generation.

sources.title: Block Diffusion: Interpolating Between Autoregressive and Diffusion Language Models
sources.summary: Combines autoregressive blocks with within-block diffusion for flexible length and cache-friendly decoding.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Bound concurrent local work and synchronize it with a stable surrounding context.
metadata.limitations: Token blocks are not lexical or AST scopes.

sources.title: Beyond Masks: Efficient, Flexible Diffusion Language Models via Deletion-Insertion Processes
sources.summary: Formulates deletion and insertion as discrete diffusion operations to support variable-length self-correction without padded masks.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Prefer explicit variable-size edit actions to pretending every hole has a fixed capacity.
metadata.limitations: The repository does not reproduce the paper's score objective or dynamic program.

sources.title: Multi-Block Diffusion Language Models
sources.summary: Matches multi-block inference with bounded groups of independently noised blocks and a static buffer implementation.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Train on the heterogeneous bounded work-set that decode will actually encounter.
metadata.repo_relevance: Active-scope buffer and independent scope noise
metadata.limitations: OpenUI scopes are non-contiguous tree regions, unlike consecutive language blocks.

sources.title: Syntax-Directed Variational Autoencoder for Structured Data
sources.summary: Uses syntax-directed attributes to constrain generation with both grammatical and semantic information.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Carry inherited and synthesized scope attributes through generation.
metadata.limitations: ScopeDiff is not a VAE and does not reproduce stochastic lazy attributes.

sources.title: Hazelnut: A Bidirectionally Typed Structure Editor Calculus
sources.summary: Defines edit actions on typed incomplete terms with holes that remain statically meaningful.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Represent holes with expected context instead of treating masks as untyped blanks.
metadata.limitations: OpenUI contracts are pragmatic schema interfaces, not Hazelnut's formal calculus.

sources.title: Constrained Decoding of Diffusion LLMs with Context-Free Grammars
sources.summary: Reduces multi-region diffusion infilling under a CFG to additive completion and language-intersection checks.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Use hard completion support for multiple holes when practical.
metadata.repo_relevance: Local wrapper oracle and final grammar verifier
metadata.limitations: The initial implementation reuses OpenUI validation and does not implement the paper's CFG intersection algorithm.

sources.title: Bidirectional Type Slicing
sources.summary: Derives minimal program slices sufficient to reproduce synthesized or expected type information, including erroneous programs.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Localize repair supervision to the smallest dependency-closed failure cone.
metadata.limitations: The implemented cone is an OpenUI dependency approximation, not the paper's mechanized type-slicing theory.

sources.title: Diffusion On Syntax Trees For Program Synthesis
sources.summary: Applies iterative diffusion edits directly to grammar syntax trees while maintaining syntactic validity.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Keep the denoising state on typed syntax topology rather than raw token spans.
metadata.limitations: The repo does not reproduce inverse-graphics execution feedback or the paper's search procedure.

sources.title: Synchromesh: Reliable code generation from pre-trained language models
sources.summary: Constrains partial program generation using syntax, scope, typing, and contextual semantic rules.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Separate hard local admissibility from learned proposal scores.
metadata.limitations: The repo does not implement Target Similarity Tuning or Synchromesh CSD.

sources.title: PICARD: Parsing Incrementally for Constrained Auto-Regressive Decoding from Language Models
sources.summary: Rejects inadmissible next tokens by incrementally parsing partial generated programs.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Fail closed on proposals that cannot extend to a valid program.
metadata.repo_relevance: Existing constrained parser and local oracle
metadata.limitations: PICARD is autoregressive SQL decoding, not multi-scope diffusion.

sources.title: Concept Embedding Models: Beyond the Accuracy-Explainability Trade-Off
sources.summary: Learns high-dimensional concept representations that remain interpretable and support intervention.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: A learned contract representation may complement explicit symbolic fields.
metadata.limitations: ScopeDiff does not implement a concept bottleneck or inherit its explainability claims.

sources.title: SAIF: A Sparse Autoencoder Framework for Interpreting and Steering Instruction Following of Language Models
sources.summary: Uses sparse-autoencoder features to analyze and steer instruction-following behavior.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: SAE steering is a possible residual critic only after symbolic contracts are validated.
metadata.limitations: No SAE is implemented or treated as a semantic oracle.

sources.title: Do Sparse Autoencoders Identify Reasoning Features in Language Models?
sources.summary: Finds that apparent reasoning features can track superficial cues and argues for causal falsification before behavioral attribution.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Do not make SAE activations authoritative without counterexamples and causal tests.
metadata.limitations: The paper evaluates different models and reasoning tasks, so only its falsification warning transfers.

sources.title: Abstract Syntax Networks for Code Generation and Semantic Parsing
sources.summary: Generates abstract syntax trees with decoder structure that follows the output grammar dynamically.

metadata.scope_diff_takeaway: Condition child generation on its parent field and schema role.
metadata.limitations: ScopeDiff is not an ASN modular decoder and does not inherit its benchmark results.
